nav.moreHint: Tip: you can reorder items in Settings → Navigation.

today.title: Today
today.mealsComingText: No plan yet. Generate this week and the app will suggest meals for your calorie targets.
today.placeholderSubtitle: Today's calories and meals will appear here.

planScreen.pastNotice: Past days can't be edited – you can only mark retroactively whether a meal was eaten.
planScreen.conflictAllergen: Contains {{allergen}}, which a profile is allergic to.
planScreen.conflictAvoided: A profile has this item marked as avoided.
planScreen.copyYesterdayConfirmMessage: This overwrites today's meals with yesterday's plan, except any already eaten.

library.placeholderSubtitle: Recipe and ingredient database with nutrition data.
library.noResults: Nothing found. Try adjusting your search or filters.
library.pantryOnlyFilter: Recipes from what's in my pantry

shopping.emptyList: The shopping list is empty for now.
shopping.emptyPantry: The pantry is empty for now.
shopping.prefillStapleResult: Added {{added}} staples to the shopping list ({{skipped}} already stocked or listed)

progress.dietCycleBanner: You've lost a good amount of body fat since you started – consider switching to maintenance calories for 1–2 months before continuing the deficit.
progress.noDataText: Once you log your first weight entry, you'll see your trend here.
progress.tdeeInsufficientData: Not enough data yet for a trend-based estimate (log your weight and meals more consistently).

walkthrough.page1Title: One plan for the whole family
walkthrough.page1Body: Everyone gets their own portions and goals, built from a single shared meal plan, pantry and shopping list.
walkthrough.page2Body: We compute your daily calories and macros (TDCI) from your details – it recalculates instantly whenever anything changes.
walkthrough.page3Title: A full week, planned for you
walkthrough.page3Body: Generate a whole week of meals in one tap, respecting allergies, diets and how often you want a recipe to repeat.
walkthrough.page4Body: The shopping list is built from your plan automatically, and tracks what you already have at home.
walkthrough.page5Body: Log your weight and body composition over time and get nudged toward maintenance at the right moment.
walkthrough.page6Body: Paste a recipe link from any site and the app pre-fills the ingredients and steps for you to review.

wizard.compositionSubtitle: A household shares one meal plan, one pantry and one shopping list.
wizard.preferencesSubtitle: These apply to the whole household; each member can still add their own on top.
wizard.maxRepetitionsHint: How many times the same recipe may appear in one weekly meal plan.
wizard.profileSubtitle: Let's set up this profile — allergies and diet preferences are set per member.
wizard.doneSubtitle: Your household is ready. You can fine-tune anything later in Settings.
wizard.compositionMismatchMessage: You originally set {{adults}} adults and {{children}} children, but created {{actualAdults}} adults and {{actualChildren}} children. Continue anyway?

form.name: Name
form.bodyFat: Body fat (optional, recommended for a more precise calorie calculation)
form.sharesMainMealsHint: Turn off to give this member an independently generated plan.
form.macroDayOverridesHint: Tap a day to adjust its protein, fat share, or surplus. An empty field falls back to the setting above.
form.workoutDaysHint: On training days the app adds a bit more calories (as carbs); on rest days it trims them back so the weekly average matches your target.
form.goalWeightConflict: With a lower target body fat, the goal weight cannot exceed the current weight.
form.recompositionHint: Missing your current body fat % - without it the app can't detect recomposition (building muscle while losing fat) and will set a plain calorie surplus instead. Fill in your current body fat % above for a more accurate result.

activityInfo.sedentary: Desk job, little or no exercise.
activityInfo.light: Light exercise 1–3×/week, or a job with some walking.
activityInfo.moderate: Exercise 3–5×/week, or a job on your feet.
activityInfo.active: Hard exercise 6–7×/week, or a physically demanding job.
activityInfo.very_active: Physical job plus very hard exercise, or athletic training.

activityQuestion.title: How active are you?
activityQuestion.hint: Include your training — this is your overall activity, exercise included.

customTdee.label: Maintenance calories
customTdee.hint: The app skips the activity-based estimate and computes your deficit/surplus and macros straight from this number.

bodyFatChart.title: Body fat ranges
bodyFatChart.hint: Approximate ranges (ACE classification). For a more precise value, use calipers or a DEXA scan.

goalDescriptions.lose: Reduce body fat through a calorie deficit
goalDescriptions.maintain: Keep your current weight and body composition
goalDescriptions.gain: Build muscle in a calorie surplus (or at maintenance if also leaning out)

dietDescriptions.vegetarian: No meat or fish; dairy and eggs are fine.
dietDescriptions.mediterranean: Olive oil, vegetables, whole grains, fish, and legumes; less red meat.
dietDescriptions.balanced: A general mix of all food groups with no specific restriction.
dietDescriptions.keto: Very low carb, high fat - carbs typically under 10% of calories.
dietDescriptions.paleo: Whole foods only - no grains, legumes, dairy, or refined sugar.
dietDescriptions.low_fat: Keeps fat intake noticeably below a typical diet.
dietDescriptions.whole30: A strict 30-day reset excluding sugar, alcohol, grains, legumes, and dairy.
dietDescriptions.fodmap: Limits fermentable carbs that commonly trigger digestive discomfort.
dietDescriptions.low_carb: Reduced carbohydrates, less strict than keto.

profile.manualAdjustmentHint: Manually nudge the computed calories up or down, in steps of 100 kcal.
profile.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}'s profile? You'll be able to undo this briefly.

navy.title: Body fat calculator
navy.subtitle: Navy tape method (body circumferences, ±3–4 % accuracy). A more precise DEXA or caliper value can be entered manually.

tdciMode.fatFloorViolated: Note: with the current protein target, there isn't enough left for the 20% fat floor - consider lowering the protein target or adjusting calories.

foodEdit.gramsPerCup: Weight per cup (optional, drives the recipe kitchen equivalent)
foodEdit.cameraPermissionNeeded: Scanning a barcode needs camera access.
foodEdit.productNotFoundMessage: This barcode wasn't found in the Open Food Facts database. You can fill in the details manually - the barcode will still be saved with this food.

foodDetail.deleteMessage: This food will be removed from the library.
foodDetail.needsReviewBanner: This food's data hasn't been reviewed yet - allergens especially may be incomplete. It won't be suggested to anyone with an allergy until you confirm it.
foodDetail.needsReviewBannerEdit: This food's data hasn't been reviewed yet. Check the allergens below, then save to confirm it.

recipeDetail.deleteMessage: This recipe will be removed from the library.
recipeDetail.printFailed: Couldn't open the print dialog. Please try again.
recipeDetail.ratingConflictMessage: One household member likes this recipe, another doesn't. How should meal planning handle it?

todayMeal.deleteMessage: It will be removed from this day's plan.
todayMeal.deleteOthersMessage: This recipe also appears {{count}} more time(s) later in the plan.

recipeImport.title: Import recipe from the web
recipeImport.errorNotFound: No recipe could be found on that page. Try a different address, or enter the recipe manually.
recipeImport.errorNetwork: The page could not be loaded. Check the address and your internet connection.
recipeImport.guideStep1: Open the recipe in your browser (e.g. on your favorite recipe site).
recipeImport.guideStep2: Tap Share and pick this app – or copy the page address (URL) manually.
recipeImport.guideStep3: A shared recipe loads automatically; if you pasted the link, tap Load recipe.
recipeImport.partialNote: The page didn't contain complete recipe data – only the name and photo were loaded.

notifications.weighInBody: Don't forget to log your weight this morning.
notifications.planningBody: Generate next week's meal plan and top up the shopping list.
notifications.shoppingBody: Check the shopping list before you head out.

settings.title: Settings
settings.maxRepetitionsHint: Household default – an individual recipe can override this limit in its own settings.
settings.allowConsecutiveDaysHint: Useful for batch cooking – the same meal may repeat the very next day.
settings.coldDinnerFrequencyHint: How many dinners per week the app tries to make cold (recipes/foods marked "can be served cold"). 0 = off.
settings.allowSameLunchDinner: Allow the same recipe for lunch and dinner
settings.allowSameLunchDinnerHint: Useful when you cook a big batch once and eat it twice the same day.
settings.preferPantryItems: Prefer what's already in the pantry
settings.preferPantryItemsHint: Nudges the meal plan toward recipes using ingredients you already have, especially ones close to expiring.
settings.mealVarietyHint: How strongly the plan pushes toward recipes you haven't had recently, versus repeating known favorites.
settings.slotsSumWarning: Enabled slots currently add up to {{sum}} %
settings.macroOverridesHint: Per-member overrides for protein, fat share and gain surplus. Leave blank to use the computed defaults.
settings.navigationHint: Drag to reorder. The first {{max}} items sit in the main bar, the rest in the expand panel.
settings.scrollMemoryHint: By default every screen resets to the top when you switch to it. Turn this on to restore your last scroll position instead, if you come back quickly.
settings.disclaimer: Built by a hobbyist, with AI assistance, for personal and family use. This is not a medical device, and its calorie, macro, and health suggestions are not medical advice. Consult a doctor or registered dietitian for medical or nutritional guidance, especially if you have a health condition.
settings.mealTimesHint: Shown when meal reminders are enabled.
settings.slotPortionsHint: Adjust how this profile splits their daily calorie target across slots. Leave unchanged to use the household default.

app.title: App & device
app.hint: Units, language, appearance, notifications, navigation and scroll memory.

household.title: Household
household.hint: Household name, diet, cuisines, foods to avoid, members and planning rules.

profile.title: Profile
profile.hint: Personal details, goals, calories & macros, slot portions and water intake.

about.title: About
about.hint: App version, onboarding replay and licence information.

householdCarousel.mealPrepModeHint: The plan will only pick main meals tagged as good for batch-cooked boxes.
householdCarousel.cardCookingExperienceHint: Sets which recipe difficulty the plan draws from automatically. You can still add other recipes yourself or mark them as favorites.
householdCarousel.cardCookingTimeHint: The plan will only pick recipes at or under this prep time.
householdCarousel.cardBudgetHint: Meal plans will respect your grocery budget.
householdCarousel.cardNotificationsHint: Get reminded about meals, your weekly weigh-in, and the shopping list.

cookingExperience.mediumHint: Balanced home meals with simple techniques.
cookingExperience.hardHint: More complex recipes and techniques too.

tempo.title: How fast?
tempo.hintLose: A faster pace means a bigger calorie deficit - the app keeps it within a safe range.
tempo.hintGain: A faster pace means a bigger calorie surplus.
tempo.slowProLose: Best preserves muscle, most sustainable pace.
tempo.recommendedProLose: The evidence-backed middle of the safe range.
tempo.fastConLose: Rates above ~1%/week are linked to losing muscle alongside fat (Garthe et al., 2011).
tempo.slowProGain: Minimal fat gain, the leanest way to build muscle.
tempo.recommendedProGain: A balanced muscle-to-fat gain ratio for most people.
tempo.recommendedConGain: Some fat gain should be expected.
tempo.fastProGain: Maximizes your growth signal, especially early in training.
tempo.fastConGain: More of the surplus ends up as fat - muscle gain is capped by training response, not just food.

mealSlots.title: How many meals a day?
mealSlots.hint: Pick every meal you actually want - skip any of them, including main meals. "Shared" means you'll eat the same recipe as the rest of the household on that slot.
mealSlots.recommendHint: 3–6 meals a day works — meal count by itself doesn't change results. For muscle goals it helps to spread protein across at least 4 meals.
mealSlots.deleteConfirmMessage: This removes {{name}} for everyone in the household who has it enabled.

addMeal.title: Add a meal
addMeal.positionPrompt: Where do you want to add it?
addMeal.countLow: Very few meals can make it harder to hit your macro targets.
addMeal.countHigh: More than 6 meals a day isn't usually necessary, but you can keep going.

water.title: Track your water intake?
water.hint: The daily goal is computed automatically from weight; you can change it anytime in settings.
water.benefitMetabolismBody: Even mild dehydration can measurably slow your metabolic rate.
water.benefitEnergyBody: Losing as little as 1-2% of body weight in fluid is linked to fatigue and lower focus.
water.benefitRecoveryBody: Good hydration supports digestion and joints, and helps you tell thirst apart from hunger.

goalReview.title: Goal review
goalReview.realisticBody: A well-supported, clinically meaningful target most people can reach and maintain.
goalReview.ambitiousBody: Achievable, but expect several months of sustained consistency.
goalReview.challengingBody: A significant transformation - success and long-term maintenance rates drop noticeably at this scale in outcome research. Consider a longer timeline or medical guidance.
goalReview.paceWarning: This pace is above the ~1%/week safe band — muscle loss becomes likely (Garthe et al., 2011).

summary.title: Summary
summary.recompositionNote: Recomposition - calories at maintenance, not a deficit.

pantry.title: What is Pantry?
pantry.body: Pantry keeps track of what your household already has at home, so meal planning and the shopping list take it into account.

pantryExpiring.title: Use up soon
pantryExpiring.body: These items are getting close to their use-by date - a gentle nudge to use them before anything else, not a warning.

prefillStaples.title: Stock up on staples
prefillStaples.body: Adds long-lasting basics (rice, oil, canned goods…) to your shopping list for a batch purchase, based on what you don't already have.

settingsApp.title: App & device settings
settingsApp.body: Units, language, appearance, notifications, navigation layout and scroll memory - anything about how the app itself behaves.

settingsHousehold.title: Household settings
settingsHousehold.body: Shared preferences for everyone in the household: diet, cuisines, foods to avoid, members and meal-planning rules.

settingsProfile.title: Profile settings
settingsProfile.body: Personal details for this profile - goals, calories and macros, slot portions and water intake.

profileChip.title: Switch profile
profileChip.body: Tap the pill to switch whose calories, macros and plan you're viewing - everyone still shares the same meal plan, pantry and shopping list.

macros.title: Your daily target
macros.body: This is your daily calorie and macronutrient target, computed from your profile - it updates automatically whenever your details change.

toleranceStatus.title: How close to target
toleranceStatus.body: Shows how closely today's planned meals match your calorie and macro target. Some day-to-day variation is normal - this isn't a pass or fail grade.

streak.title: Your streak
streak.body: Counts consecutive days you logged this. A missed day just pauses the streak - it doesn't erase your progress, so there's no need to start over.

bodyFat.title: Reference ranges
bodyFat.body: These bands show a typical range for a given age and sex, based on population data - not an "ideal" or a goal to hit.

waterGoal.title: How the goal is set
waterGoal.body: Your daily water goal is estimated from your weight. You can set your own number anytime in Settings → Profile.
